findings.summary: Across the corpus's five-year span nobody is hired, promoted, or leaves: the same Director, Senior Associate and Analyst staff all four engagements under identical titles, which leaves Ryan Strong an Analyst five and a half years after joining a firm that has no other analyst to be junior to.
findings.evidence: d:0001 (2020-05-31): 'Sandra Perez, Director, leads the engagement day to day... Briana Dennis, Senior Associate, builds the model and drafts the report. Ryan Strong, Analyst, prepares the comparable-company work'. d:0016 (2024-06-29), four years later: 'Sandra Perez, Director, will run the engagement day to day... Briana Dennis, Senior Associate, will prepare the working drafts... Ryan Strong, Analyst, will build and tie out the supporting schedules.' d:0017 (2024-10-12) still lists 'Ryan Strong, Analyst'. foundation.json: every employment.end is null and no start is later than 2019-04-06. Per rf:voice-1 these four letters share no common format, so the identical title assignment is the roster speaking, not a reused template. In financial advisory, Analyst to Associate runs two to three years; an Analyst still titled Analyst at 5.5 years has been promoted or has left.

findings.summary: The Managing Partner is a signature and a review promise only, never present in a working session, status report or readout across four engagements, which is how a partner behaves at a 500-person firm rather than at a five-person boutique.
findings.evidence: Michael White appears in 5 of 17 authored documents (mention_map.json): the four engagement letters plus the firm overview. He is in none of the four sets of minutes (d:0005, d:0012, d:0014, d:0017), no status report, no deck, no email. d:0007 (2022-04-30) claims 'Every engagement is partner-led from proposal through readout, and nothing leaves the office that a partner has not read' -- and the firm has exactly one partner -- yet the only readout in the corpus, d:0011 (2022-07-18), states 'Midpoint client briefing prepared for [client] by Fernhollow Partners LLC. Presented by Sandra Perez, Director.'

findings.summary: One of the three declared client organizations and two of the four external contacts never appear in any document, so the firm's documented client base across five years is two companies and two people.
findings.evidence: mention_map.json has zero entries for x:perez-kemp, xp:jeffery.waller and xp:theresa.waller. All four engagements in engagements.json go to Woods LLC or Nguyen, Reynolds and Martinez. Perez-Kemp is declared org_type 'client' in foundation.json with no engagement, no letter, and no mention anywhere; Jeffery Waller (VP Finance, Perez-Kemp) and Theresa Waller (General Manager, Nguyen, Reynolds and Martinez) are roster entries no reader of the corpus can ever encounter. No defect claimed in the prose: a 19-document sample need not cover every relationship. Recorded because a third of the declared external graph is inert.
